context: BlarAI vision-trailer dev-side prototype (docs/handoffs/trailer-music-capability-handoff-20260711.md) — local MusicGen generation trial informing the #858 Cinematic Studio music-tier decision. Scratch tooling only (scratchpad/gen_music.py); not a BlarAI runtime capability.

hardware.gpu: Arc 140V (Xe2) — NOT used, this run is the CPU-only leg

methodology: Single 15s clip, seed=42, guidance_scale=3.0 (default), do_sample=True, prompt='epic cinematic trailer score, sweeping orchestral strings, heroic brass, building percussion, tension and triumph'. Model loaded fresh (cold cache already warm on disk from a prior download); one generation call, no batching.

comparison_prior.musicgen_small_cpu: prior dev-side smoke test (not re-measured to this rigor) suggested roughly ~2 s-audio/min-compute on the same box; medium is markedly slower, consistent with its larger parameter count

implication: At 0.83 s-audio/min-compute, a full ~90s cinematic track would cost roughly 108 minutes of CPU compute — impractical for iterative use. The Arc/OpenVINO GPU export path (optimum-cli export openvino --model facebook/musicgen-<tier> --weight-format int8, then device=GPU) is required for production-viable generation of the full track, not merely an optimization.
not_measured: musicgen-medium (or any tier) on Arc 140V via OpenVINO INT8/INT4
musicgen-large tier (fits disk at ~13GB but not attempted here, CPU impractical)
perceptual quality A/B judgment (operator's call, sample delivered same session)
